jokes.text: Brought to you by Mutual of Omicron. Insuring your world, and its destruction.
jokes.punchline: Insurance company casually mentions they also insure planetary destruction
jokes.explanation: Strong opener that establishes the show's satirical tone with efficient dark humor about insurance covering apocalypse

jokes.text: I'm born, baby!
jokes.setup: Documentary about salmon lifecycle beginning with hatching
jokes.punchline: Fry's characteristically enthusiastic reaction to being born
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry character moment - his excitement about mundane things translates well to being literally born

jokes.text: What's your name? I don't have a name. I'm a salmon.
jokes.punchline: Deadpan reminder that fish don't have names
jokes.explanation: Good character work with Bender's matter-of-fact delivery cutting through Fry's human assumptions

jokes.text: I do look forward to cavorting in the spray.
jokes.punchline: Professor's overly formal language about simple fish behavior
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor - using unnecessarily sophisticated vocabulary for basic animal instincts

jokes.text: Let's all 10 of us go together. There's safety in numbers.
jokes.setup: Documentary just said only 3 in 10 survive the journey
jokes.punchline: Character optimistically suggests all 10 go together despite doom
jokes.explanation: Nice dramatic irony - audience knows the mortality rate but characters remain naively optimistic

jokes.text: I reckon my boy will be right pleased when I regurgitate that into his mouth.
jokes.setup: Bird discussing feeding habits while hunting salmon
jokes.punchline: Zapp's casual mention of regurgitation as parental affection
jokes.explanation: Zapp's confident delivery makes disgusting natural behavior sound inappropriately paternal

jokes.text: It sure is relaxing getting swept along by this current.
jokes.punchline: Character finding the deadly situation relaxing
jokes.explanation: Good ironic contrast between peaceful description and impending doom

jokes.text: Oh, my! Oh, dear! Oh, yes!
jokes.setup: Salmon going over waterfall to their deaths
jokes.punchline: Final exclamation sounds inappropriately pleasurable
jokes.explanation: Dark humor in making death sound orgasmic, though relies on innuendo

jokes.text: This just in. Three dead in foamy plunge.
jokes.punchline: Clinical news report delivery about nature documentary deaths
jokes.explanation: Excellent use of Morbo's news anchor persona to treat documentary deaths as breaking news

jokes.text: Still, six out of 10 alive. That's considered good for our species!
jokes.punchline: Fry's cheerful optimism about terrible survival rate
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry character work - finding the bright side in horrific mortality statistics

jokes.text: Five out of 10, also nice.
jokes.punchline: Death toll increasing but still framed positively
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the death count joke, maintaining toxic positivity

jokes.text: Except the salmon.
jokes.setup: Ocean is birthplace of all life
jokes.punchline: Dry correction about salmon being freshwater spawners
jokes.explanation: Efficient correction that punctures the grandiose nature documentary language

jokes.text: Really? Me, too! / No way!
jokes.setup: Two salmon meet and identify as salmon
jokes.punchline: Acting shocked to discover they're the same species
jokes.explanation: Absurd human social behavior applied to fish. Simple but effective character comedy.

jokes.text: So, hey, I don't want to be too forward, but I'd like to mate with you in a few years when we're sexually mature.
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly direct mating proposition framed as politeness
jokes.explanation: Great cringe comedy - treating animal mating instincts like awkward human dating

jokes.text: Also Pisces. We're all Pisces.
jokes.punchline: All fish are Pisces by definition
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels - astrology meets marine biology

jokes.text: You haven't ignored the last of me.
jokes.punchline: Zapp's malapropism mixing 'seen the last' and 'heard the last'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp moment - confident delivery of completely mangled threat

jokes.text: Nobody knows!
jokes.setup: Question about how salmon navigate home
jokes.punchline: Professor's enthusiastic admission of scientific ignorance
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor catchphrase deployed perfectly - scientific mystery met with gleeful ignorance

jokes.text: Okay, everybody do one of those three things.
jokes.setup: Three possible navigation methods listed by narrator
jokes.punchline: Practical instruction to pick any random method
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor - treating mysterious instinct like a multiple choice question

jokes.text: I can tell by the magnets in my head, or whatever.
jokes.setup: Explaining how he knows which stream he's from
jokes.punchline: Bender's casual dismissal of his own navigation system
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender - having actual magnetic navigation but treating it dismissively

jokes.text: Stupid body! Why won't you go to that other stream? Nobody knows! I just told you!
jokes.setup: Amy fighting against salmon instinct, Professor repeats catchphrase
jokes.punchline: Amy's frustrated response to Professor's unhelpful interjection
jokes.explanation: Great callback usage - Professor's catchphrase becomes actively unhelpful, escalating Amy's frustration

jokes.text: I'll never forget you, fish.
jokes.setup: Parting with female salmon he can't mate with
jokes.punchline: Generic farewell using species name instead of individual identity
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Fry's attempt at romance undermined by calling her 'fish'

jokes.text: Come on, foxy lady, it's time for us to get naughty by nature!
jokes.punchline: Mixing 'foxy lady' with nature pun and sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Solid Bender line with nature pun, though relies on obvious sexual innuendo

jokes.text: Son of a fish!
jokes.punchline: Fish-appropriate version of profanity
jokes.explanation: Clean pun that adapts human profanity to fish context effectively

jokes.text: Jumping is for suckers.
jokes.setup: Other salmon struggling to jump upstream
jokes.punchline: Zapp's arrogant dismissal of essential salmon behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp arrogance - dismissing instinctual behavior as beneath him

jokes.text: I'm good! Who wants a piece of me? I'll try a bite!
jokes.setup: Bender boasting after making it upstream
jokes.punchline: Bear immediately accepting the challenge literally
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup/payoff - Bender's bravado immediately punished by predator taking him literally

jokes.text: Okay, who's next? There's a lot more where that came from!
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate boasting about reproductive capacity
jokes.explanation: Moderately funny cringe humor about inappropriate sexual boasting, somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: I can't see them anymore! What if they're mating? Who cares? Just release your milt already!
jokes.setup: Fry worried about his love interest mating with others
jokes.punchline: Leela's practical advice about fish reproduction
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic - Fry's romantic ideals vs Leela's biological realism

jokes.text: I can't see them anymore! What if they're mating? / Who cares? Just release your milt already! / Everybody's doing it!
jokes.setup: Salmon trying to time mating properly
jokes.punchline: Peer pressure about releasing fish sperm
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy treating biological function with teenage peer pressure dynamics.

jokes.text: Tell my offspring I loved me very much...
jokes.punchline: Self-referential final words instead of loving his offspring
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character-specific joke - Fry's narcissism creates perfect dying words malapropism

jokes.text: Oh, yeah. Plop those roundies.
jokes.punchline: Bender's crude commentary on egg-laying
jokes.explanation: Typical Bender crudeness applied to fish reproduction, somewhat lazy but in character

jokes.text: I am Brrr, devourer of fish and honey!
jokes.punchline: Lrrr's grandiose introduction modified for bear context
jokes.explanation: Excellent character adaptation - Lrrr's alien ruler persona perfectly translated to bear

jokes.text: That is what you said when you found those Boy Scouts!
jokes.setup: Bear defending eating during spawning season
jokes.punchline: Wife references previous overeating incident with humans
jokes.explanation: Dark humor escalation - bears eating Boy Scouts treated as marital spat

jokes.text: Next you'll be telling me I can't crap in the woods!
jokes.setup: Marital argument about bear's eating habits
jokes.punchline: Famous saying referenced literally by actual bear
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reversal - famous rhetorical question used literally by the animal it refers to

jokes.text: And so the endless circle of life comes to an end, meaningless and grim. Why did they live, and why did they die? No reason.
jokes.punchline: Nihilistic inversion of typical 'circle of life' message
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of nature documentary tropes - replacing wonder with existential dread

jokes.text: Also the blue-footed booby! That's fairly interesting! Note the blue coloration of the...
jokes.punchline: Professor's interruption and abrupt cutoff about obvious detail
jokes.explanation: Good Professor moment - interrupting with obvious observation that gets cut short

jokes.text: Sad news, everyone. I'm lonesome.
jokes.punchline: Professor's matter-of-fact delivery of tragic situation
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor delivery - announcing species extinction like routine news

jokes.text: Would it? Would it? Would it?
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's desperate enthusiasm for any attention
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg characterization - desperate for any form of interaction or purpose

jokes.text: Hey, good lookin', I've got a lichen that needs a lickin'.
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate pickup line with lichen/licking pun
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay combining tortoise biology with crude pickup line

jokes.text: Talk about a cloaca shrinker.
jokes.punchline: Anatomically specific expression of disappointment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant crude humor using actual reptile anatomy for relatable expression

jokes.text: It's only 100 feet! At my age, it might as well be 200 feet. I'd never make it!
jokes.setup: Journey being too far for old tortoise
jokes.punchline: Professor's escalation makes short distance sound impossibly far
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke - doubling an already tiny distance to emphasize old age

jokes.text: Don't be such a finch.
jokes.punchline: Species-specific insult replacing 'chicken'
jokes.explanation: Clever adaptation of common insult to Galapagos setting

jokes.text: I'm not Fry. I'm his great-great-grandson, Fry. You are? My beak is different, see? It evolved a slightly bigger hook over the generations to help me eat the cactus on this part of the island.
jokes.setup: Tortoise thinking it's the same finch after 100 years
jokes.punchline: Fry explaining evolution while looking identical
jokes.explanation: Brilliant evolution joke - character looks identical but claims evolutionary change

jokes.text: I'm stuck! Oops.
jokes.setup: Demonstrating evolved beak for cactus eating
jokes.punchline: Immediately getting stuck, proving evolution failed
jokes.explanation: Perfect physical comedy payoff - evolutionary 'improvement' immediately backfires

jokes.text: Oh, my darling! You haven't aged a day!
jokes.setup: Hubert reuniting with his love after 100 years
jokes.punchline: She's actually a skeleton/fossil
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark visual gag with perfect setup/payoff structure, subverts romantic reunion expectation.

jokes.text: Oh, my darling! You haven't aged a day!
jokes.setup: Hubert reunites with female tortoise after 100 years
jokes.punchline: She's actually dead - a skeleton
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with good ironic setup. Standard 'hasn't aged' compliment becomes grimly literal.

jokes.text: Should we say something? Nah. Let him have his fun.
jokes.punchline: Characters deciding to let the delusion continue
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor about allowing delusion to continue, extends the skeleton gag nicely.

jokes.text: Who is that hard-shelled skank? I'll kill her!
jokes.setup: Female tortoise finding Hubert with another
jokes.punchline: Aggressive jealousy using turtle anatomy in insult
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific jealousy incorporating turtle shell into insult

jokes.text: I'm going to crack that hussy open like a slutty Brazil nut!
jokes.punchline: Mixing turtle shell cracking with nut metaphor and slut-shaming
jokes.explanation: Creative threat combining turtle biology with nut cracking metaphor

jokes.text: Like all reptiles, the Galapagos tortoise is cold-blooded.
jokes.punchline: Narrator's dry biological correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect documentary correction undercutting romantic characterization with biology

jokes.text: Should we be watching this? It seems kind of personal. That's not an issue for us. We're wildlife.
jokes.punchline: Wildlife don't have privacy concerns
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-commentary on nature documentary voyeurism

jokes.text: Is it weird if I talk about his crazy turtle penis? No.
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact acceptance of crude wildlife commentary
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to wildlife privacy joke, though relies on anatomy humor

jokes.text: What do you want? I'm 150 years old.
jokes.punchline: Age as excuse for parental neglect
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Professor's selfishness justified by extreme age

jokes.text: I sure as hell don't.
jokes.punchline: Blunt rejection of maternal instincts
jokes.explanation: Effective deadpan delivery of parental abandonment

jokes.text: Mama!
jokes.punchline: Calling for mother who just abandoned them
jokes.explanation: Dark irony - baby seeking parent who explicitly won't help

jokes.text: For, in the end, nature is horrific, and teaches us nothing.
jokes.punchline: Complete nihilistic rejection of learning from nature
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of nature documentary wisdom - embracing meaninglessness as the lesson

jokes.text: Aw, yeah! Survival of the fattest!
jokes.punchline: Darwin reference modified for blubber-based success
jokes.explanation: Clever evolution pun that works both for seals and Bender's personality

jokes.text: Aw, yeah! Survival of the fattest!
jokes.punchline: 'Survival of the fattest' instead of 'fittest'
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay on evolutionary concept, character-appropriate boasting, highly quotable.

jokes.text: Whoo! I'm doin' it in my sleep!
jokes.punchline: Bender's crude boast about effortless mating
jokes.explanation: Standard Bender sexual boasting, in character but not particularly creative

jokes.text: Hmm. I don't know, does this kelp scarf make me look fat enough?
jokes.setup: Lesser male's inability to attract mates
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg worried about not being fat enough
jokes.explanation: Excellent reversal of typical body image concerns - wanting to be fatter, not thinner

jokes.text: I'm 40% ass! Arf, arf!
jokes.setup: Challenge about having an ass under blubber
jokes.punchline: Bender's signature percentage claim with seal sound
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender callback adapted to seal context - his '40%' gag with appropriate animal sound

jokes.text: No one ever goes walrus.
jokes.punchline: Immediate deflation acknowledging walrus undesirability
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-aware deflation of classic pickup line format

jokes.text: Kill all penguins... Kill all penguins.
jokes.punchline: Creature has inexplicable hatred for unrelated species
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor with random species hatred, quotable but somewhat disconnected.

jokes.text: Yeah, we can always make more. Come on, floozies, let's repopulate!
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal of infanticide followed by mating call
jokes.explanation: Dark Bender humor treating baby death casually, though somewhat predictable

jokes.text: Hurray! We're three friends at the beach!
jokes.punchline: Fry's innocent misinterpretation as friendship
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry innocence - completely missing sexual context for wholesome friendship

jokes.text: When we're in charge, we'll treat females with respect, keeping sensible harems of no more than five.
jokes.setup: Beta males planning revolution for female respect
jokes.punchline: Defining respect as smaller harems rather than equality
jokes.explanation: Excellent satirical humor about male feminism, perfectly captures missing-the-point mentality.

jokes.text: He's been dead since yesterday! What? He's dead? Whoo! I'm killin' dudes in my sleep! Yo! Clean up!
jokes.punchline: Bender fought dead opponent, celebrates killing in sleep
jokes.explanation: Good dark escalation - Bender's violent pride enhanced by absurd circumstances

jokes.text: For a holographic brain injection of tonight's program, send $5 to this station, care of me, the narrator.
jokes.punchline: Futuristic viewing option with personal profit motive
jokes.explanation: Good sci-fi parody of PBS fundraising with narrator's self-interest
